name: Antithymocyte immunoglobulin (rabbit)
description: Rabbit anti-thymocyte globulin. Thymoglobulin is a polyclonal antibody that suppresses certain types of immune cells responsible for acute organ rejection in transplant patients. Thymoglobulin is a mixture of antibodies intended to bind to various cell surface antigens. The most common mode of action of Thymoglobulin is via selective depletion of T-cells.
indication: For prevention of renal transplant rejection
toxicity: Not known whether ATG (rabbit) distributes into human milk; however, other immunoglobulins are distributed into human milk.
mechanismOfAction: Binds to multiple, T-cell specific antigens leading to T-lymphocyte cell death via complement mediated cytotoxicity or apoptosis.
pharmacodynamics: Antithymocyte Globulin (ATG) is a concentrated anti-human T-lymphocyte immunoglobulin preparation derived from rabbits after immunization with a T-lympoblast cell line. ATG is an immunosuppressive product for the prevention and treatment of acute rejection following organ transplantation. ATG reduces the host immune response against tissue transplants or organ allografts.

interactions.name: Natalizumab
interactions.description: The risk or severity of adverse effects can be increased when Antithymocyte immunoglobulin (rabbit) is combined with Natalizumab.

interactions.name: Pimecrolimus
interactions.description: The risk or severity of adverse effects can be increased when Pimecrolimus is combined with Antithymocyte immunoglobulin (rabbit).

interactions.name: Tofacitinib
interactions.description: Antithymocyte immunoglobulin (rabbit) may increase the immunosuppressive activities of Tofacitinib.

interactions.name: Tacrolimus
interactions.description: The risk or severity of adverse effects can be increased when Tacrolimus is combined with Antithymocyte immunoglobulin (rabbit).

interactions.name: BCG vaccine
interactions.description: The therapeutic efficacy of BCG vaccine can be decreased when used in combination with Antithymocyte immunoglobulin (rabbit).
